title: Unit Final: Foundations of Digital Citizenship

questions.prompt: Which statement best defines digital citizenship?

choices.text: Responsible, ethical, and safe use of technology
choices.feedback: Correct. Digital citizenship includes behavior, responsibility, and safety online.
This is too limited. Digital citizenship involves more than entertainment use.
Ownership does not define digital citizenship.

choices.text: The ability to access the internet anywhere
choices.feedback: Access is part of digital citizenship, but not the full definition.

questions.prompt: Digital citizenship only applies when you are using social media platforms.

choices.feedback: Incorrect. Digital citizenship applies to all online and digital interactions.
Correct. Digital citizenship applies across all digital environments.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an example of digital inclusion?

choices.feedback: This restricts access rather than promotes inclusion.

choices.text: Ensuring everyone has equal access to digital tools and resources
choices.feedback: Correct. Inclusion focuses on equitable access.

choices.text: Using only one device for all users
choices.feedback: This does not ensure equal or effective access.

choices.text: Limiting internet access to certain groups
choices.feedback: This goes against digital inclusion.

questions.prompt: A student excludes a classmate from an online group project chat. What concept does this most directly relate to?

choices.feedback: Security involves protecting information, not participation.
This refers to online presence, not access.
Etiquette involves behavior, but this scenario focuses more on inclusion.
Correct. Excluding someone limits their participation and access.

questions.prompt: Using respectful language online is part of digital etiquette.

choices.feedback: Correct. Respectful communication is a key part of digital etiquette.
Incorrect. Respectful language is essential to proper online behavior.

questions.prompt: Which response demonstrates appropriate digital etiquette?

choices.feedback: This violates privacy and etiquette.

choices.text: Posting a rude comment when you disagree
choices.feedback: This violates digital etiquette.

choices.text: Responding respectfully even when you disagree
choices.feedback: Correct. Respectful communication is key.
This does not demonstrate engagement or etiquette.

questions.prompt: A student receives a mean comment online and feels upset. What is the most responsible first step?

choices.text: Respond with an equally mean comment
choices.feedback: Escalating conflict is not responsible behavior.

choices.text: Share the comment publicly to embarrass the sender
choices.feedback: This escalates the situation and is not appropriate.
Ignoring may not always be the best solution.

choices.text: Pause and decide whether to report or block the user
choices.feedback: Correct. This shows responsible decision-making.

questions.prompt: Thinking before posting online can help prevent digital conflicts.

choices.feedback: Correct. Thoughtful posting reduces misunderstandings and conflict.
Incorrect. Thinking ahead is key to responsible digital behavior.

questions.prompt: Which scenario best demonstrates responsible digital decision-making?

choices.text: Forwarding a message without verifying it
choices.feedback: This can spread misinformation.

choices.text: Taking time to evaluate the impact before sharing content
choices.feedback: Correct. Responsible decisions require careful thinking.

choices.text: Reacting immediately to every online message
choices.feedback: Impulsive reactions can lead to problems.
This is unsafe and irresponsible.

questions.prompt: During an online discussion, two students strongly disagree. What is the best approach to manage the conflict?

choices.feedback: Avoiding the situation may not resolve the issue.
Reporting is appropriate only if behavior violates guidelines.

choices.text: Respectfully explain your perspective and listen to theirs
choices.feedback: Correct. This promotes constructive conflict resolution.

choices.text: Insult the other person to prove your point
choices.feedback: This escalates conflict and is inappropriate.
